title: Fuzz Testing of Closed-Source Software Using Large Language Models
data:
TD Ovasapyan, DA Gavrichkov, DV Ivanov- Automatic Control and Computer, 2025
The architecture of large language models (LLMs) and the possibility of their 
application to automate fuzz testing software are studied. As a result of the study, a 
method is developed that enables fuzz testing applications with a graphical interface 
in the Windows operating system. A software prototype implementing the proposed 
method is developed and tested.
Cites: Large language model guided protocol fuzzing

title: Automated Method for Evaluating the Correctness of Distributed Algorithms Using Chaos Engineering Testing
data:
AD Dakhnovich, AA Lyrchikov, DA Moskvin- Automatic Control and Computer, 2025
This article presents a method for evaluating the correctness of distributed algorithms 
using chaos engineering methods to improve the efficiency of their testing. Modern 
research studies are analyzed in the field of testing distributed systems, such as 
federated learning systems for large language models (LLMs) and chaos

analyzed and their shortcomings are identified. As a result, a method for evaluating
